blocks.text: Pricing Strategy: Garza, Leblanc and Porter

items: Client of record: {{fact:f:E-2009-004.client}}
Engagement commenced {{fact:f:E-2009-004.start}}
Prepared for Barbara Ward, Director of Operations
Prepared by Mary Parker, with Deborah Gordon and Brenda Rodriguez
An early-phase briefing, not a set of conclusions

blocks.text: The question we are here to answer

items: Why the prices you set on paper are not the prices you collect
Where your margin is strong and where it quietly erodes
How prices are set today, and who along the way may discount
What a pricing approach you can actually run would look like
From your own invoice and rebate data, not from rules of thumb
Every figure traceable to a workpaper we can produce on request
Findings and recommendations kept separate and clearly labeled
Interim reads shared with you as they firm up, not held to the end

blocks.text: What we can see so far

Two things stand out even at this early stage, and both need more work before we would put a number on either. The gap between what you list and what you actually collect is real and worth measuring precisely. And the discounting is not spread evenly: it concentrates in the largest accounts, several of which carry allowances that never reach the standard price sheets. We offer these as directions to look, not as conclusions.
What we are still working through

items: Reconciling two years of rebates and allowances against the invoice records
Interviews on who quotes and who may discount, now getting under way
How the off-sheet allowances on the major accounts were first agreed
Close out the realization and margin diagnostic
Finish the pricing-authority interviews with your sales managers
Bring a first set of findings, labeled as findings, to the next session
Keep routing data requests through Barbara Ward's office
